arrival: You drop anchor at a harbor built from crates — thousands of them, each with a number painted on the lid.
Captain Nullptr taps one crate and it opens instantly. "A good pirate never searches," he says. "She indexes."
The dockmaster keeps a ledger: every cargo gets a key, every key points straight to its crate.
Learn the ledger's trick — hash the name, jump to the spot — and no treasure on this island can hide from you.
arrays-hashing.boss_intro: The Warehouse Leviathan surfaces, ten thousand unsorted crates rattling on its back!
arrays-hashing.complete: The dockmaster stamps your papers. You now see every array as a harbor and every value as a labeled crate.
arrays-hashing.title: Keeper of the Ledger

arrival: Two lighthouses guard this island — one at each end of a single long beach.
Their beams sweep toward each other, crossing the sand exactly once, missing nothing.
"Why walk the beach twice?" Nullptr asks. "Start at both ends. Meet in the middle."
Some nights a second, faster beam laps the slow one — that's how the keepers catch smugglers running in circles.
two-pointers.boss_intro: The Two-Headed Pointer Serpent rises from the shallows, one head fast, one head slow!
two-pointers.complete: The lighthouse keepers salute. You'll never brute-force a sorted shoreline again.
two-pointers.title: Twin-Beam Navigator

arrival: From the crow's nest, the lookout scans the horizon with a long brass spyglass.
She never re-scans what she's already seen — she slides the glass one degree, drops the old edge, adds the new.
"The sea is too big to look at twice," she says, tossing you the spyglass.
Keep what's inside the frame, let go of what leaves it, and the widest ocean becomes one smooth pass.
sliding-window.boss_intro: The Fog Colossus advances — its shifting bulk can only be measured one sliding frame at a time!
sliding-window.complete: The lookout pins her spyglass to your coat. One pass is all you need now.
sliding-window.title: Spyglass Sentinel

arrival: This island is a single towering column of dinner plates, huts balanced on top of huts.
Supplies go up one at a time, and the only way down is whatever went up last.
"Last in, first out," grumbles the quartermaster. "Like my regrets."
Match what opens with what closes, and the tower never topples.
stack.boss_intro: The Plate Golem stacks itself skyward — topple it one perfectly matched plate at a time!
stack.complete: The tower stands. You leave knowing exactly what to push, what to pop, and when.
stack.title: Warden of the Tower

arrival: The island's great library holds a million charts, and the librarian answers every request in twenty steps.
She opens the middle drawer, glances once, and discards half the library without apology.
"Sorted shelves are a promise," she says. "Break the problem in half, then break the half."
Twenty honest halvings beat a million guilty guesses.
binary-search.boss_intro: The Half-Blind Kraken attacks — it can only be struck in the exact middle of its remaining reach!
binary-search.complete: The librarian hands you a bookmark shaped like a cutlass. Halve everything, always.
binary-search.title: Halver of Seas

arrival: No solid ground here — just barrels roped together in one long floating chain.
Each barrel knows only one thing: which barrel comes next.
"Lose a rope, lose the fleet," warns Nullptr, re-tying a knot with three fingers.
Walk the chain, reverse the chain, splice the chain — but never let go of the barrel you're holding.
linked-list.boss_intro: The Chainbreaker Eel coils through the barrels, snapping ropes k links at a time!
linked-list.complete: The barrel-folk cheer. You can now re-rope a drifting fleet in your sleep.
linked-list.title: Ropemaster of the Drift

arrival: A single colossal tree covers this island, each branch splitting into exactly two smaller branches.
Fruit-pickers shout their positions: some call out floor by floor, others dive root-to-leaf and back.
"Every branch is a smaller tree," says Nullptr. "Solve the twig, and you've solved the trunk."
Climb with recursion in one hand and a queue in the other.
trees.boss_intro: The Rootmind awakens — a tree so deep its leaves have never heard news from the trunk!
trees.complete: The canopy parts to let your ship's mast through. Branches hold no fear for you now.
trees.title: Canopy Climber

arrival: The harbor tavern seats a hundred captains, yet the loudest bounty always sits at the front table.
When one leaves, the crowd shuffles just enough to push the next-loudest forward.
"Nobody sorts the whole tavern," the barkeep shrugs. "We only ever care who's first."
Keep the best on top and let the rest stay comfortably messy.
heap.boss_intro: The Tavern Titan bellows — face the patrons in exactly the order of their fury!
heap.complete: The barkeep engraves your name on the front table. Priority, always.
heap.title: First Table Champion

arrival: A hedge maze of black coral sprawls from shore to shore, and the paths rearrange at dusk.
The island's children race in holding chalk: mark, explore, and if the path sours — erase and step back.
"Wrong turns aren't failures," Nullptr says. "They're map data."
Try everything once, keep nothing that fails, and the maze runs out of tricks.
backtracking.boss_intro: The Maze Regent seals the exits — only one arrangement of your steps can dethrone it!
backtracking.complete: Chalk in hand, you sign the maze's guestbook on the first try. Eventually.
backtracking.title: Chalkline Explorer

arrival: A grove of word-trees: every path from root to leaf spells a sailor's name, letter by letter.
Shared beginnings share branches — a thousand names, one trunk.
"Store the prefix once," whispers the grove-keeper, "and every word that starts with it comes free."
tries.boss_intro: The Lexicon Wyrm slithers through the grove — hunt every hidden word on its scaled board!
tries.complete: The grove-keeper carves your name into the trunk. It shares a branch with 'grand'.
tries.title: Prefix Warden

arrival: An archipelago of tiny islets, tangled together by rope bridges in no particular order.
The ferry-folk explore two ways: flood outward ring by ring, or dive one bridge deep until it dead-ends.
"Mark where you've been," warns the ferryman, "or the bridges will walk you in circles."
Some bridges have rules — cross this one before that one — and the wise pirate lines them up first.
graphs.boss_intro: The Bridge Baron cuts the archipelago in two — flood every islet before the tide beats you!
graphs.complete: Every islet flies your flag. Connected components, conquered.
graphs.title: Baron of Bridges

arrival: Beyond the archipelago the currents themselves have prices, and every route costs coin or time.
Harbor pilots argue: cheapest path, shortest path, the one path that uses every bridge exactly once.
"Out here, walking isn't enough," says Nullptr. "You must walk cleverly, with a ledger open."
Weigh every edge. Trust the mathematics, not the wind.
advanced-graphs.boss_intro: The Compiler King's Admiral blockades the strait — decode the order of its alien signal flags!
advanced-graphs.complete: The pilots' guild presses their golden compass into your hands.
advanced-graphs.title: Pilot of Weighted Waters

arrival: A staircase of ancient stone climbs the island's only mountain, and every step has a toll.
The toll-keeper remembers every climber who came before — she never charges the same question twice.
"The future is built from answers you already paid for," she says, tapping her ledger.
Solve the small step, write it down, and the mountain assembles itself beneath you.
dp-1d.boss_intro: The Ledger Wraith demands the one partition that balances its cursed scales!
dp-1d.complete: The toll-keeper waves you through free. Memo written, mountain mastered.
dp-1d.title: Toll-Keeper's Heir

arrival: Here the farmland is a giant grid, and every field's harvest depends on its two upwind neighbors.
Farmers fill their tables corner to corner, never planting a cell before its sources are known.
"One dimension was practice," smiles Nullptr. "Now the ledger has rows AND columns."
Build the table patiently. The bottom-right corner is where the treasure sums.
dp-2d.boss_intro: The Grid Sovereign unrolls an infinite scroll — match its regular expressions or be erased!
dp-2d.complete: The farmers harvest in your honor. No grid will ever look infinite again.
dp-2d.title: Sovereign of the Table

arrival: The market island runs on one law: take the best deal in front of you, right now.
Merchants who hesitate to reconsider old trades are politely thrown into the bay.
"Greed works," says the harbormaster, "but only when today's best choice can never ruin tomorrow."
Learn to smell which bargains are safe to grab — and which need a ledger instead.
greedy.boss_intro: The Auction Fiend floods the docks with brackets and wildcards — bid perfectly or bust!
greedy.complete: The merchants nickname you 'One-Pass'. It's meant with respect.
greedy.title: One-Pass Magnate

arrival: The final island before open water: a harbor scheduling a thousand overlapping departures.
The harbormaster merges berths, cancels collisions, and still finds room for every honest ship.
Veterans gather here too, trading endgame tricks — bit-flips, digit spells, geometry sleights.
"Everything you've learned sails with you now," says Nullptr. "Clear the schedule. The One Solution waits."
intervals-endgame.boss_intro: The Timetable Tyrant slams the harbor gates — answer every query with the tightest interval or stay ashore forever!
intervals-endgame.complete: The First Sea lies conquered behind you. Ahead, through the fog, the Blind Sea waits.
intervals-endgame.title: Admiral of the First Sea

arrival: The Blind Sea swallows your charts. No patterns are written on these waters.
A bell buoy clangs somewhere in the fog — the first isle takes shape.
Here they time your cutlass. Draw fast, think faster.
blind-isle-1.boss_intro: A shadow with too many masts blocks the channel!
blind-isle-1.complete: First isle cleared. The fog respects you a little more.
blind-isle-1.title: Fogcutter

arrival: The second isle drifts — it was not here on anyone's map, because there are no maps.
Interviewer gulls circle overhead, timing your every move.
Trust the instincts the First Sea gave you.
blind-isle-2.boss_intro: The gulls part for something bigger!
blind-isle-2.complete: Second isle cleared. Your instincts are sharpening.
blind-isle-2.title: Gullwatcher

arrival: Three bells toll. The third isle rises like a question you've heard before, worded differently.
The fog hides the pattern, but your hands already know it.
Solve first. Name the trick later.
blind-isle-3.boss_intro: The water boils — a boss with no silhouette!
blind-isle-3.complete: Third isle cleared. Patterns whisper through the fog now.
blind-isle-3.title: Bellcounter

arrival: Halfway through the Blind Sea, the crew stops asking where you're going.
They've seen you stare into gray nothing and pull answers out of it.
The fourth isle tests whether that was luck.
blind-isle-4.boss_intro: A rival captain steps from the mist, stopwatch in hand!
blind-isle-4.complete: Fourth isle cleared. It wasn't luck.
blind-isle-4.title: Mistwalker

arrival: The fifth isle is loud — waves argue with rocks, and the clock argues with you.
Somewhere in the noise is a quiet, familiar structure.
Find it before the timer finds you.
blind-isle-5.boss_intro: The rocks themselves rearrange into a snarling gate!
blind-isle-5.complete: Fifth isle cleared. You hear structure inside noise now.
blind-isle-5.title: Stormlistener

arrival: On the sixth isle, old enemies return wearing new fog.
You've beaten every one of them before, in brighter water.
Prove that mastery survives the dark.
blind-isle-6.boss_intro: A ghost-lit hull you could swear you've sunk before!
blind-isle-6.complete: Sixth isle cleared. Old victories, new depths.
blind-isle-6.title: Rematch Reaper

arrival: The seventh isle barely exists — a sliver of rock and one leaning signpost with no words.
This close to the end, the Blind Sea stops pretending to be fair.
Good. You stopped needing fair four isles ago.
blind-isle-7.boss_intro: The signpost bends — the isle itself is the ambush!
blind-isle-7.title: Signless Sailor

arrival: The last isle of the Blind Sea, and beyond it — a glow the fog cannot smother.
Every timer, every star, every scar led here.
The Compiler King's flagship waits. So does The One Solution.
blind-isle-8.boss_intro: The Compiler King's flagship emerges — all sails, all cannons, zero comments!
blind-isle-8.complete: The Blind Sea kneels. The One Solution is yours — it compiles everywhere, forever. Legend.
blind-isle-8.title: Sovereign of the Blind Sea
